name: Modin (Parallel Pandas)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); UC Berkeley; Ponder.io commercial product

performance.notes: Parallelizes pandas across all CPU cores; 5-20x speedup depending on operation

agent_readiness.notes: Drop-in pandas replacement that parallelizes operations across all CPU cores. `import modin.pandas as pd` — no other code changes. Ray or Dask execution backend. 5-20x speedup for data operations. Not all pandas operations supported (falls back to pandas gracefully). Handles larger-than-memory data via Ray/Dask. Best for ETL pipelines where pandas is the bottleneck. Polars often faster for new code but Modin requires zero code changes.

gotchas: Not all pandas operations supported (graceful fallback to pandas)
Overhead for small DataFrames (< 1M rows pandas is faster)
Some operations slower than expected due to coordination overhead
best_when: Speeding up existing pandas code without rewriting (zero code change)
Large DataFrame operations (10M+ rows) on multi-core machines
ETL pipelines where pandas is a bottleneck
avoid_when: New code (Polars is faster and more memory-efficient)
When Dask/Ray adds too much infrastructure
summary: Modin is a drop-in pandas replacement that parallelizes operations across all CPU cores. Change one import line — no other code changes needed. Ray or Dask backend. 5-20x speedup for large DataFrames. Best for existing pandas code that's too slow. For new code, Polars is typically faster; for larger-than-memory data, Dask scales better. Modin excels when you need speed with zero code changes.
